summary: Updated pulse.py and init_build.py to support build_mode: learning with manual spawn default and rush override. Added get_build_mode() helper defaulting to 'standard' for backward compat, new 'ready' status for learning-mode manual-launch drops, build_mode-aware spawn logic in tick (rush=auto, explicit spawn_mode takes precedence, mechanical engagement_tag=auto in learning mode, learning default=manual), rush command with per-Drop/per-Wave/per-Build scope, and STATUS.md/show_status display of ready drops. init_build.py now sets build_mode: learning and learning_landscape_generated: false in every new meta.json. All existing builds without build_mode default to 'standard' (auto-spawn), preserving backward compatibility.
decisions: Defaulted get_build_mode() to 'standard' (not 'learning') for backward compatibility — existing builds without a build_mode field behave exactly as before (all auto-spawn)
Used 'ready' status for learning-mode manual drops vs existing 'awaiting_manual' for explicit spawn_mode:manual — semantically distinct states
In the manual spawn branch, only set status to 'ready' when build_mode is 'learning'; otherwise use existing 'awaiting_manual' for explicit per-drop manual override
Manual-spawn drops don't set spawn_worker_pid, spawn_requested_at, or register_drop_conversation since no worker process is created
concerns: The 'ready' and 'awaiting_manual' statuses serve very similar purposes — future cleanup could unify them, but for now they're semantically distinct (learning vs explicit manual override)
Rush mode at build level sets build_mode='rush' permanently — there's no 'un-rush' to return to learning mode (could add if needed)
notes_for_orchestrator: Test with both learning-mode and rush-mode builds. Verify backward compat with builds that have no build_mode field. The rush command resets 'ready' drops back to 'pending' so the next tick picks them up for auto-spawn.
